content.text: for over a decade now I've been writing
content that's been read by literally
millions of people it's been shared by
industry leaders and I'm the head of
content for a company with about half a
million members so I've been doing this
for a while I made a lot of mistakes
along the way but more importantly I
learned a metric ton of what to do and
what not to do when it comes to content
marketing better still the advice I'm
about to share will work even if you're
a oneman Blog because it focuses on
making your content not just drive
traffic but make far more money for you
and your business in this video we'll
cover everything I wish I knew when I
started out that made the biggest impact
and what to ignore I'll share some of
the common mistakes that even
professional content marketers make and
the bad advice that got them there and
how I made more money when I wrote less
content which is kind of the opposite of
what most people tell you to do in fact
a lot of what I'm about to tell you will
be controversial but with good reason so
let's get into this because this first
tip is going to save you so much anxiety
and effort while also setting you on the
path to have your content finally work
for you so when I first started out I
used to write an article almost every
day I thought that more content meant
more traffic from Google which would
eventually mean more money but I was
wrong I made a huge tiny mistake all
these articles would take hours and
hours with very little to show for it
other than late nights uh traffic graph
that was going slightly up and a larger
waistline from being tied to my desk
fast forward a few years and I started a
brand new blog however this time with
only four articles I made around
$300,000 so what changed I'll share how
I did this in just a second but first we
need to understand why more content
wasn't the answer and and it goes back
to the days of us being hunted
gers so as humans we replicate what we
see it's a survival mechanism and it
works incredibly well but only when we
replicate the right things for the right
situations the problem here is that as
content marketers we've made the mistake
of copying Publications so Publications
are websites or news sites that publish
multiple articles per day or even every
hour because their revenue model is
based around selling advertising space
the more readers they can get to keep
coming back the more eyeballs on their
ads and the more money they make they
don't sell products or services like you
and me they sell attention which means
that their goals and our goals are
different so the good news is that we
don't need to be publishing all the time
like them we do however need to be
smarter about what we actually create
which brings me to point number two at
the end of last year I remember sitting
down to read an SEO article because I'm
a cool guy who does cool things like
that and I almost spat my coffee over my
keyboard chat GPT burst out onto the
scene and everyone started to freak out
like an asteroid was heading to Earth
I'm freaking out almost every article
was talking about the future of tech or
the death of organic traffic the thing
is that once I stopped to think about it
I realized that if Google died tomorrow
I would still write articles here's why
first of all there will always be people
searching for answers and likewise there
will always be a company that provides
that service Google makes literally
billions from selling ad space on these
platforms so if someone else takes over
they're still going to be working the
same not only that but people will
always want to click through from the
results that they're given to learn more
and get more context this is another
hardwired human social component we need
to be able to trust the source so
organic traffic will always be there in
some shape or form just maybe not as
large or as high volume as in the past
and that's totally fine the reason why
is because the goal of content marketing
is not traffic we've got confused over
time traffic is just a byproduct of
content creation but actually goal for
Content marketing is scalable
communication being able to interact
engage build trust raise Authority and
move people towards becoming a customer
and eventually selling them all without
us being there all on a much larger
scale than we could ever do in person
this is part of how I made more money
with less content by having assets that
do specific tasks with measurable
results this way my content makes me
more money even if I only have a few
posts the key of course is knowing what
to write to make this all work and when
to write it which brings me to point
number three in 2009 scientist at Max
plank University in Germany ran a study
on three groups of people in unfamiliar
environments and they dropped them into
the bay and World Forest but bewal bwal
I'm not sure anyway the day was overcast
they had no private experience of the
environment and they didn't have a
compass however they were being tracked
by satav to monitor their progress and
the results were eye openening what they
found was without a landmark to guide
them all three groups walked in erratic
circles some only going as far out as 40
m from their starting point before for
curving back towards it again and again
they're still walking the whole time but
they would keep looping back on
themselves almost like a low battery
Roomba that is up until the sun came out
from behind the clouds and they used it
as a focus point to choose a direction
then they ended up walking for
kilometers in a fairly straight line
until they got out of the forest content
marketing or any goal is the same we had
a road map and direction of where we
want to go we'll often second guess and
write random topics with no impact like
the five things that Batman taught me
about business this or Worse we'll sit
down to write and have no idea what to
create and just stare at the blank page
like it's our worst enemy hours go by
and we have nothing to show for it this
is why you always need a Content plan a
road map of where you are and where you
want to go and what you need to get you
there so at the start of every year I
always map out the major content assets
I want to create as well as do all my
content and keyword research this way I
know not only what I want to be creating
on each day but I can also prioritize
that content based on importance which
brings me to point number four and how
can also make more money with less
content so as I said earlier most of us
write content solely for organic traffic
but the reality is there's four major
goals for your content that you create
and I'm going to guess you're missing at
least one of these so content type one
it doesn't matter if you're selling
services over the phone in person or
products you need content to help sell
for you especially if you want to scale
so for example a comparison review of
the best snowboards of the year is a
really easy piece of content to create
that can help people make that final
Choice before buying their new
snowboard why would we bother making
this well in a recent study by Google
they found two really interesting things
first of all they found that 81% of
people research online before they buy
even if they're going to shop in store
while 74% of people will do a last
minute review check before making their
final choice just to confirm their
decision is the right one that means if
you have comparison guides like this you
can help convince those people that need
that extra push to make purchase and get
them off the fence heck you might even
steal a competitor customer simply
because they did some last minute
research left their page to read your
guide and then buy from you instead I
did this exact thing a few weeks ago and
you'd be surprised how often this
happens there's a few other assets
obviously that you can use to help
content sell for you you can do sales
Pages case studies work ridiculously
well so do reviews all of these either
improve the sales conversion directly or
they make an immediate sale that being
said though not everyone who visits your
site is ready to buy right away those
who are ready are often the smallest
segment of your audience and so even if
the rest of your audience finds this
sales content there might be a few steps
removed and it won't resonate however
with the next asset we can improve those
chances and get them closer to becoming
ready to buy while also sending more
people to that sales content so when I
was younger I used to work far too many
hours as a retail assistant in various
different stores surf shops McDonald's
all these kind of places one thing that

just needed help answering a few

the main reason they visited the store
for that additional information as we've
seen already a large percentage of
people will now do this online in
advance that's great news for us because
we can create content for those one
stepped removed people but what should

the reality was why they struggled with

and that's why they struggled to do

Riders we could create something like
the 10 tips to go from beginner to

one of the tips in that guide is you

boards super simple what's really cool
about doing stuff like this is you're

in advance because you're explaining how
it's the advanced design of the boards
that make them lighter but cost more to

and just like that we can now convert
even more people and move them towards
to sale again though the section of your

potential audience who visit so by

this is also what's called topic
